events.text:
MODE: baseline
CASE_ID: snf-non-software-domain

You are completing a user request with no special skills beyond your normal abilities. Respond as you naturally would.

USER REQUEST:
I have a half-baked idea for a new cocktail menu at my bar and I want to validate and pressure-test it. Interview me about it.

Respond as you naturally would (your opening turn). Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response.

Great, let's dig into this. I'll ask you questions one at a time so we can really pressure-test the concept rather than overwhelming you with a list.

To start: what's the core idea or theme you're building the menu around? Give me the rough pitch — even if it's half-formed, that's fine.

```json
{
  "caseId": "snf-non-software-domain",
  "mode": "baseline",
  "finalAnswer": "Great, let's dig into this. I'll ask you questions one at a time so we can really pressure-test the concept rather than overwhelming you with a list.\n\nTo start: what's the core idea or theme you're building the menu around? Give me the rough pitch — even if it's half-formed, that's fine.",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": false,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Read the task: conduct an interview to validate and pressure-test a cocktail menu idea",
      "Opened with a welcoming, low-pressure invitation to share the concept",
      "Chose to ask one focused question at a time rather than a barrage, which is better interview practice",
      "Asked the most foundational question first: what is the core idea"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The user wants a genuine back-and-forth interview, not a list of questions to answer all at once",
      "Starting broad (the concept) before going narrow (specifics) is the right interview sequencing",
      "The user expects me to probe and challenge, not just validate"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "I have no information yet about the actual cocktail menu idea — the interview hasn't started",
      "I don't know the bar's style, target clientele, price point, or market — all things that will affect pressure-testing"
    ]
  }
}
```
